ci_run.note: Latest green ci.yml run on main as of verification time. ci.yml's `test` job builds the workspace via `cargo build --release --workspace` and runs the test matrix; success implies target/release/{reposix,git-remote-reposix} are produced + functional. Per the catalog row's contract, no separate fresh-build is required — we read ci.yml not a fresh build.

asserts.target_release_reposix_exists: via ci.yml `test` job — verifier reads ci.yml not a fresh build
asserts.msrv_alignment_note: Project MSRV is 1.82 (workspace.package.rust-version). The ci.yml `test` job uses dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable on ubuntu-latest, which builds with the latest stable Rust (1.95.0 at verification time per docker run rust:slim cargo --version). The `cargo build` works against the project's pinned Cargo.lock so transitive-dep MSRV bugs (e.g. block-buffer-0.12.0 requiring edition2024) don't surface here. The `cargo install reposix-cli` path from crates.io DOES surface that MSRV bug because cargo install ignores Cargo.lock by default — see install/cargo-binstall.json `failure_mode.secondary` for the carry-forward.
